franchise_agreement_structure.effective_date: Date of franchisor countersignature + payment of IFF and Boot Camp Fee

grant_and_term.grant: Non-exclusive right to operate EOS franchise as EOS Implementer using System, IP, and Marks
grant_and_term.term: 5 years ending last day of calendar month following 5-year anniversary
grant_and_term.renewal: Up to two 3-year terms. Must sign then-current FA (may be materially different). General release required.
grant_and_term.extension: Franchisor may extend term by up to 12 months if franchisee is in good standing to allow processing of renewal or to facilitate franchise operations
grant_and_term.implementer_requirement: Franchisee must serve as sole Implementer. 51%+ ownership of Operating Entity required.

fees_and_payment.monthly_membership: $1,395–$1,695 based on tier, may increase up to $200/year
fees_and_payment.brand_fund: Up to $100/mo if established (60 days notice)
fees_and_payment.technology_fee: Up to $100/mo if established (30 days notice)
fees_and_payment.speaking_referral: 25% of gross revenue from referred engagements
fees_and_payment.manner_of_payment: ACH or credit card on file. Franchisor may debit account.
fees_and_payment.no_set_off: Franchisee has no right to set off amounts owed against any claims
fees_and_payment.interest_on_late: 1.5%/month or max by law, accruing 30 days after due date

training_obligations.boot_camp: Mandatory. 27 hours, 3 days. Must complete within 6 months. Failure = termination right.
training_obligations.remedial_training: May be mandated by franchisor at franchisee expense ($995–$10,000) for breaches, performance failures, or quality concerns
training_obligations.qce_attendance: Minimum QCE attendance required for tier maintenance. May be required in-person.
training_obligations.continuing_education: Must complete online training courses from time to time

operational_obligations.franchise_system_standards: Must operate in strict conformance with Operations Manual and Franchise System Standards
operational_obligations.modifications: Franchisor has unlimited right to modify System Standards. Franchisee must implement at own expense.
operational_obligations.record_keeping: Must maintain full, complete, accurate records. Quarterly session reporting required.
operational_obligations.minimum_performance: Minimum session days, session fees, QCE attendance, ratings (8.75+) required per tier. Rolling 12-month measurement. Failure triggers Cure period (2 quarters), then potential remedial training, then potential termination.
operational_obligations.client_satisfaction: Must participate in Client Satisfaction Program — cannot charge for sessions where client reports insufficient value
operational_obligations.insurance: CGL $1M/$1M, E&O $1M/$1M. Must maintain throughout term. 5 business days to cure lapse.
operational_obligations.best_efforts: Must use best efforts to promote business. Must refrain from acts that bring Marks into disrepute.

technology_obligations.computer_system: Must have computer <3 years old with current OS and browser
technology_obligations.practice_management: Must use franchisor CRM system for session reporting
technology_obligations.email: Must use issued EOS email for all franchise business correspondence
technology_obligations.websites: Cannot establish separate website using EOS IP without consent
technology_obligations.social_media: Must comply with brand guidelines for all social media
technology_obligations.ai_usage: Section 8.6 addresses AI usage — terms in Operations Manual

termination_triggers.by_franchisee: 60 days written notice (or 10 days before Boot Camp). Must execute release, pay fees through termination.
non_curable_defaults: Brand-damaging misconduct (including past conduct becoming public)
Failure to meet minimum performance after remedial training
3+ defaults in any term (even if cured)
Failure to commence business within prescribed time

transfer_restrictions.definition: Any voluntary/involuntary assignment, sale, gift, pledge, mortgage, or other disposition of any interest in FA, material assets, or ownership
conditions: Transferee meets all qualifications + passes background check
All amounts paid to franchisor, affiliates, third-party creditors
Transferee signs then-current FA for remainder of term
Transferee completes Boot Camp at current fees
General release by transferor and co-owners
Franchisor approves material terms of transfer
If seller-financed, subordinate to franchisor's rights
90-day transition support if requested by franchisor

during_term.scope: Cannot own, operate, or have interest in any competing business worldwide
during_term.competing_business_definition: Any business that directly or indirectly owns/operates non-EOS business operating system, provides training for non-EOS systems, provides outsourced entrepreneurial coaching similar to EOS, or otherwise competes with franchisor — anywhere in the world
during_term.exceptions: Passive 5% or less ownership of publicly traded company

restrictions: Cannot solicit EOS clients to competing business
Cannot induce strategic partners, referral sources to affiliate with former franchisee
Cannot interfere with franchisor business relationships
Cannot engage in acts prejudicial to franchisor goodwill
post_term.carve_out: May continue non-EOS services for existing clients where such services were provided during franchise term

noncompete.non_disparagement: Cannot make statements (including social media) that impair goodwill of Marks, System, franchisor, affiliates, or other franchisees

dispute_resolution.mediation: Non-binding, Michigan. At franchisor's sole discretion. AAA Commercial Mediation. Sole neutral mediator with 5+ years franchise law experience. Costs split equally (mediator fees); each party bears own costs.
dispute_resolution.arbitration: Binding, Michigan. At franchisor's sole discretion. AAA Commercial Arbitration. Neutral franchise attorney with 5+ years experience. Delaware substantive law applied. One 7-hour deposition, 10 interrogatories, reasonable document requests. Prevailing party gets full cost reimbursement.
dispute_resolution.governing_law: Delaware (except federal law for trademarks and copyrights)
dispute_resolution.injunctive_relief: Franchisor may seek from any court without bond (or $1,000 bond if court requires)
dispute_resolution.punitive_damages_waiver: Both parties waive punitive and exemplary damages to fullest extent permitted by law
dispute_resolution.jury_trial_waiver: Both parties irrevocably waive jury trial
dispute_resolution.class_action_waiver: Both parties waive class action rights
dispute_resolution.prior_notice_requirement: Franchisee must notify franchisor within 90 days of violation/breach as condition precedent to damages claim
dispute_resolution.limitations_period: 1 year from occurrence (except indemnification and IP claims)
dispute_resolution.prevailing_party_fees: Full reimbursement of all litigation/arbitration expenses

indemnification.by_franchisee: Must indemnify franchisor against all claims, losses, liabilities arising from franchise operations, franchisee's breach, unauthorized use of marks, employee/contractor claims, client data handling, tax obligations
indemnification.by_franchisor: Must participate in defense and indemnify franchisee for expenses/damages in administrative/judicial proceedings involving Marks/IP if franchisee used in compliance with FA and proceeding resolved unfavorably

insurance_requirements.additional_insured: Franchisor must be named as additional insured

force_majeure.description: Not explicitly addressed as a standalone clause in the FA. Franchisor may extend term up to 12 months for good standing franchisees. Leave of absence policy available for extraordinary circumstances at franchisor's discretion.

distinctive_clauses.description: Franchise Agreement includes provisions on Artificial Intelligence Usage. Terms defined in Operations Manual. Specific restrictions not visible in FDD.
Default on 3 or more separate occasions within any term, whether or not cured (including failure to adhere to Core Values), is a non-curable termination event.
All client information, mailing lists, databases are franchisor property. Franchisee may not use except for franchise operations.
All ideas, concepts, techniques developed by franchisee relating to franchise must be disclosed and assigned to franchisor. Considered works made-for-hire. No guaranteed compensation.
Cannot make statements (including social media) that impair goodwill of Marks or System. Extends to franchisee's personnel.
